summary: PR 2213 was a small configure.py-only restoration that marked several item source files as Matching again after they had been changed to NonMatching by PR #2203. The affected files were all under melee/it/items and included common items, special move/projectile items, and Pokémon-related item files. The decomp.dev bot reported a GALE01 linked-code increase of +0.41% / +15772 bytes and linked-data increase of +0.08% / +1008 bytes.

key_files.reason: Only changed file; restored Matching/NonMatching object declarations in the item object list.

what_changed: Removed the separate Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/itchicoritaleaf.c") entry from configure.py in the Pokémon item section.
smart_moves: Kept the fix narrow: no source changes, only the build/matching manifest in configure.py.
Restored known matching units directly in the Object(Matching, path) list instead of reworking item code.
Used the project’s matching status mechanism as the repair point, which made the regression easy to audit in the diff.
Preserved surrounding object ordering and comments, limiting churn to the affected item entries.
decomp_lessons: Matching status in configure.py is part of the decompilation state and can regress even when C source files are unchanged.
When a PR unexpectedly lowers linked code/data, inspect Object(Matching) vs Object(NonMatching) declarations for accidental demotions.
A config-only PR can recover measurable progress when matching units were incorrectly excluded from linked matching accounting.
For item work, keep per-item source paths stable in the object list so future matching-status diffs remain easy to review.
naming_conventions: Item source files use the it-prefixed lowercase naming convention under melee/it/items, such as itheart.c, ittomato.c, itmariocape.c, and itfushigibana.c.
configure.py represents match state with Object(Matching, "path") and Object(NonMatching, "path") entries.
The PR title uses an explicit cross-reference, "PR#2203", to identify the regression source.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly changes or function-level matching tactics are visible in this slice.
The relevant matching tactic was to restore object-level Matching declarations for files already believed to match.
The decomp.dev report showed the practical effect on GALE01 linked code/data after the manifest correction.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
decomp-dev[bot] reported for GALE01: linked code 31.03% (+0.41%, +15772 bytes) and linked data 28.88% (+0.08%, +1008 bytes).
The bot comment also included the text "No changes", but the same report recorded positive linked code/data deltas.
follow_up_queries: PR 2203 configure.py Object NonMatching items regression
